auth_notes: Requires an Integrator ID (API Key) and a Firm ID. The process involves a multi-step handshake: sending credentials and the 'Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key' to a login endpoint to receive a JWT session token, which must be passed in the 'Authorization' header for subsequent calls.
rate_limits: Rate limits are enforced via Azure API Management and vary by API product (Tax, Document, Practice) and subscription tier. Typical limits are around 10-20 calls per second.
gotchas: Obtaining an Integrator ID is a manual process through Wolters Kluwer and often requires a paid developer subscription.
The API uses complex OData-style query parameters for filtering and sorting which are sensitive to URL encoding.
Session tokens are short-lived, requiring the n8n workflow to include logic for re-authentication or token refreshing.
operations_available: Get Tax Return Status and Metadata
research_notes: The integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. A dedicated 'Auth' sub-workflow is recommended to handle the login process and return a valid token. All requests must include the 'Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key' header. Data structures are deeply nested, so significant use of the 'Set' or 'Edit Image' nodes will be required for data mapping.
